name: Auburn State Bank
description_short: Auburn State Bank is an FDIC-insured community bank in Auburn, Nebraska, offering personal and business checking accounts with online and mobile banking options.
description_long:
Auburn State Bank is a traditional community bank headquartered in Auburn, Nebraska, operating since at least the early 2000s. The bank is FDIC-insured, meaning customer deposits are backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government up to applicable limits. The bank maintains a physical branch location at 1212 J Street in Auburn, NE 68305.

Auburn State Bank offers a range of core banking products including personal checking accounts, business checking accounts, and electronic banking services. They provide online and mobile banking platforms for customer convenience, allowing account holders to manage finances remotely. The bank also offers customer support through multiple channels, including a main phone line (402-274-4342) and an after-hours emergency line (800-383-8000) for issues like lost or stolen debit cards.

As a community bank, Auburn State Bank distinguishes itself by maintaining a local physical presence and offering personal service in the Auburn area. The bank's website includes an "Insights" section focused on financial education topics relevant to consumers. They operate standard banking hours with dedicated customer service support for debit card emergencies and fraud concerns.

Auburn State Bank appears to be a straightforward community banking option with standard offerings. The website provides limited detail about specific account features, interest rates, fees, or product differentiation compared to competitors. No information is available about savings accounts, CDs, investment products, or lending services like mortgages or business loans, though these may be available. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by the U.S. Government
Physical branch location in Auburn, Nebraska for in-person service
24/7 emergency debit card fraud support line (800-383-8000)
Online and mobile banking platforms available for account management
Dual enrollment options for both consumer and commercial customers
Clear fraud prevention guidance with specific phone numbers for lost/stolen cards
Financial education resources available through Insights section
cons: Limited online presence with minimal product information or comparison details
No information about account fees, interest rates, or minimum balance requirements
No details provided about savings accounts, CDs, or investment products
No mortgage, personal loan, or business lending services mentioned on website
Single physical location limits accessibility for customers outside Auburn, Nebraska area
best_for: Auburn, Nebraska residents seeking local banking relationships
Small business owners in the Auburn area needing business checking accounts
Customers who prefer in-person banking interactions over digital-only options
Community members wanting FDIC-insured deposits with local oversight
diagnosis: Auburn State Bank is best for residents and small business owners in the Auburn, Nebraska area seeking traditional community banking with FDIC protection and local service. The primary caveat is that the bank's online presence provides minimal detail about products, rates, and fees, requiring direct contact with the bank to compare offerings against other financial institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
